title: FAOSTAT — Crops and Livestock Products (QCL), Saudi Arabia, 1961–2024
abstract: FAO's Crops and Livestock Products (QCL) domain data filtered to Saudi Arabia, covering area harvested, production quantity, yield, live-animal stocks, and slaughter/laying statistics across 86 crop and livestock items, 1961–2024. Accompanied by FAO's full domain metadata (methodology, definitions, quality notes) and supporting PDFs on unit conversions and revision notes.

variables.name: element
variables.label: Element
variables.description: Statistical measure reported: Area harvested, Production, Yield, Yield/Carcass Weight, Stocks, Laying, Milk Animals, or Producing Animals/Slaughtered.

variables.name: item
variables.label: Item
variables.description: Crop or livestock commodity (86 distinct items), e.g. Almonds in shell, Wheat, Cattle, Hen eggs.

variables.name: unit
variables.label: Unit
variables.description: Unit of the Value column (e.g. ha, t, hg/ha, head, 1000 heads); varies by element and item per FAOSTAT metadata.

variables.name: flag
variables.label: Flag
variables.description: FAOSTAT data-quality flag, e.g. 'M' = missing value; data cannot exist.

access.how_to_request: Freely downloadable from the FAOSTAT web portal under CC BY 4.0; no request required.

provenance.originator: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), Statistics Division
provenance.citation: FAO. 2026. FAOSTAT: Crops and Livestock Products (QCL) — Saudi Arabia, 1961-2024. Rome: FAO.

known_issues: FAOSTAT's own metadata reports imputation rates of 41% (2022), 45% (2023), and 52% (2024) for primary crop/livestock products, so a large and growing share of recent-year values are model-estimated rather than officially reported (see FAOSTAT_metadata.csv, section 14.3).

Limited to FAO's QCL (crops and livestock) domain — does not include FAO's fisheries/aquaculture statistics (FishStatJ), which the KAUST Sources registry lists separately as a '[Previous]' source but for which no local file was found in this folder.
National-level only; cannot be spatially attributed to the Red Sea coast versus the rest of the Kingdom.
